proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous feature feature_012 is positively correlated with pfs_months (longer feature_012 associated with longer PFS).
The ordinal feature feature_005 (values 0/1/2) is monotonically associated with pfs_months: higher feature_005 corresponds to shorter PFS.
feature_006=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006=0.
feature_013=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_013=0.
feature_001=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_001=0.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.702 (p<1e-300). feature_012 is the strongest single continuous predictor of pfs_months.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.f_oneway across feature_005 in {0,1,2}; mean PFS per stratum.
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_005=0 -> 5.640, =1 -> 4.444, =2 -> 3.289. ANOVA F=2860.8, p<1e-300. Clear monotonic decrease.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_006']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_006']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.617 (feature_006=1) vs 5.153 (feature_006=0); diff -1.536, t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 5.408 (feature_013=1) vs 4.310 (feature_013=0); diff +1.098, t-test p<1e-300.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.750 (feature_001=1, n=5090) vs 4.800 (feature_001=0); diff -1.050, p=1.45e-178.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_028=0.
feature_029=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_029=0.
feature_018=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_018=0.
feature_023=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_023=0.
Continuous feature_004 is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Continuous feature_033 is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Continuous feature_024 is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.328 (feature_028=1) vs 4.887 (feature_028=0); diff -0.559, p=6.1e-144.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_029
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.865 (feature_029=1) vs 4.297 (feature_029=0); diff +0.568, p=3.7e-143.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.317 (feature_018=1) vs 4.776 (feature_018=0); diff -0.459, p=6.1e-70.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on pfs_months by feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.824 (feature_023=1) vs 4.462 (feature_023=0); diff +0.362, p=5.8e-59.

analyses.code: pearsonr(feature_004, pfs_months)
pearsonr(feature_033, pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: r=-0.091, p=3.0e-93. Univariate effect is small but highly significant given n=50000.

analyses.code: pearsonr(feature_024, pfs_months)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Most candidate prognostic features keep their univariate sign and are independently associated with pfs_months in a joint linear model adjusting for all other features.

Several binary features (feature_011, feature_019, feature_030, feature_015, feature_020, feature_022, feature_009, feature_026, feature_032, feature_008) have negligible adjusted association with pfs_months and may not be prognostic.

analyses.code: statsmodels.OLS(pfs_months ~ all 37 features); inspect coefficients and p-values.
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable R^2 = 0.792. Strong adjusted predictors retained: feature_012 (+0.176/yr, p<1e-300), feature_006 (-1.549, p<1e-300), feature_005 (-1.171/level, p<1e-300), feature_013 (+1.101, p<1e-300), feature_001 (-0.983, p<1e-300), feature_028 (-0.582, p<1e-300), feature_029 (+0.532, p=8.6e-275), feature_024 (+0.476, p<1e-300), feature_018 (-0.471, p=2.4e-239), feature_004 (-0.077/unit, p<1e-300), feature_033 (-0.019/unit, p<1e-300), feature_031 small but significant. Notably feature_011, feature_019, feature_030, feature_023, feature_015, feature_020, feature_022, feature_008, feature_032, feature_009, feature_026, and most of the remaining lab-like continuous features (feature_002, feature_007, feature_010, feature_014, feature_017, feature_021, feature_025, feature_034, feature_035, feature_036, feature_037, feature_027) are not significant after adjustment (p>0.05 or marginal).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_030, which is essentially balanced 50/50 (mean 0.501) and has no main effect on pfs_months, is a candidate treatment-assignment indicator. Plausible if there is treatment-effect heterogeneity, the marginal effect can be zero while subgroup effects are large.
feature_011 (mean 0.447) and feature_019 (mean 0.605), which also have negligible adjusted main effects, are alternative candidate treatment indicators.

analyses.code: Comprehensive interaction screen: OLS(pfs_months ~ feature_030 + mod + feature_030:mod) for every other feature mod; sort by interaction p-value.
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions of feature_030 with: feature_036 (p=0.004), feature_006 (p=0.048), feature_007 (p=0.058). All small effect sizes; no biologically large heterogeneity. feature_030 appears not to be the primary treatment.

analyses.code: Same interaction screen with feature_011 and feature_019 as candidate tx.
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 strongest interaction was with feature_012 (p=0.019, coef -0.037). feature_019 strongest was with feature_013 (p=0.0043, coef -0.134) and feature_028 (p=0.029, coef +0.104). No huge interactions; no candidate treatment role established.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 is the active treatment indicator: although it has a strong positive marginal effect on pfs_months, its effect should differ dramatically across subgroups defined by other binary features if it is a treatment with biomarker-defined response.

analyses.code: Full interaction screen: OLS(pfs_months ~ feature_013 + mod + feature_013:mod) for every other feature.
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 has enormous interactions with: feature_028 (interact coef -1.623, p=4e-255), feature_029 (+1.594, p=9e-229), feature_033 (-0.637 per SD, p=1.5e-174), feature_018 (-1.364, p=2.2e-118), feature_023 (+1.093, p=2.6e-117), feature_016 (+0.419, p=9.3e-19). The magnitudes are larger than feature_013's marginal effect itself (+1.10), indicating strong qualitative effect modification. Definitive evidence feature_013 is a treatment with subgroup-restricted activity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is concentrated in patients with feature_028=0; in feature_028=1 patients the effect is null.

analyses.code: Stratified t-test of pfs_months by feature_013, separately for feature_028=0 and =1.
analyses.result_summary: feature_028=0 (n=32635): PFS 5.97 (tx) vs 4.31 (ctl), diff +1.66, p<1e-300. feature_028=1 (n=17365): PFS 4.35 vs 4.32, diff +0.04, p=0.29. The treatment effect is essentially zero when feature_028=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_013 is concentrated in patients with feature_029=1; feature_029=0 patients receive no benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified t-test of pfs_months by feature_013, separately for feature_029=0 and =1.
analyses.result_summary: feature_029=1 (n=34886): PFS 5.89 (tx) vs 4.31 (ctl), diff +1.577, p<1e-300. feature_029=0 (n=15114): PFS 4.29 vs 4.30, diff -0.017, p=0.63. Treatment is null when feature_029=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_013 is abolished in patients with feature_018=1; the benefit is restricted to feature_018=0.

analyses.code: Stratified t-test of pfs_months by feature_013, separately for feature_018=0 and =1.
analyses.result_summary: feature_018=0 (n=40940): PFS 5.66 (tx) vs 4.31 (ctl), diff +1.350, p<1e-300. feature_018=1 (n=9060): PFS 4.31 vs 4.32, diff -0.014, p=0.77. Treatment null when feature_018=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_013 declines monotonically with the continuous quantitative biomarker feature_033 and falls to ~0 at high values.

analyses.code: Stratify into feature_033 quartiles (whole cohort) and compute treatment effect of feature_013.
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect of feature_013 by feature_033 quartile (whole cohort): Q1 [1.0,7.5] +1.842; Q2 [7.6,12.2] +1.921; Q3 [12.3,19.6] +0.653; Q4 [19.7,100] -0.016. Clear monotonic decline; effect crosses zero around feature_033 ~17-20 in the whole cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_033 is not a prognostic marker per se but a treatment-specific effect modifier: it has no association with pfs_months in untreated patients (feature_013=0) but strongly predicts shorter PFS in treated patients (feature_013=1).

analyses.code: pearsonr(feature_033, pfs_months) computed separately within feature_013=0 and feature_013=1.
analyses.result_summary: Within untreated (feature_013=0): r=-0.002, p=0.68 — feature_033 is NOT prognostic. Within treated (feature_013=1): r=-0.220, p=9.4e-190 — feature_033 strongly predicts response. Confirms feature_033 is a predictive (treatment-effect modifier) rather than a prognostic biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Three binary modifiers act jointly: in the subgroup feature_028=0 AND feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0, the feature_013 treatment effect is large; outside that joint subgroup, the effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: Define mask = (feature_028==0)&(feature_029==1)&(feature_018==0); compute treatment effect inside vs outside.
analyses.result_summary: Inside joint subgroup (n=18681, ~37% of cohort): PFS 7.21 (tx) vs 4.30 (ctl), diff +2.908, p<1e-300. Outside (n=31319): PFS 4.319 vs 4.316, diff +0.003, p=0.91. The treatment effect is fully captured by this joint subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_033 < ~14 to the subgroup definition further sharpens the responder phenotype: in patients with feature_028=0, feature_029=1, feature_018=0, AND feature_033 < 14, feature_013 yields a much larger treatment effect than in the rest of the (broader) joint subgroup.

analyses.code: Within mask (028=0, 029=1, 018=0), bin feature_033 into deciles and compute feature_013 effect within each decile.
analyses.result_summary: Within the joint biomarker-positive subgroup, feature_013 effect by feature_033 decile: D1 [1.0,4.9]+4.92; D2 [5.0,6.7]+4.88; D3 [6.8,8.4]+5.03; D4 [8.5,10.2]+5.01; D5 [10.3,12.2]+4.89; D6 [12.3,14.6]+3.97; D7 [14.7,17.6]+0.17; D8 [17.7,22.0]-0.07; D9 [22.1,29.5]-0.23; D10 [>=29.6]+0.16. There is a clean threshold around feature_033 ~14: below it, treatment effect ~+5 months; above it, ~0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint biomarker-positive subgroup, the feature_033-by-treatment interaction is approximately linear: treatment effect declines roughly linearly with feature_033 until it reaches zero around feature_033 ~14-15.

analyses.code: OLS within mask: pfs ~ feature_013 + (feature_033-12.2) + feature_013:(feature_033-12.2).
analyses.result_summary: Within joint subgroup (n=18681): const 4.302, feature_013 +3.359 at median feature_033, feature_033 main effect ~0 (p=0.79), interaction -0.135 per unit feature_033 (p<1e-300). Linear extrapolation: treatment effect crosses 0 at feature_033 ~ 12.2 + 3.36/0.135 = ~37, but the empirical decile pattern shows the actual cutoff is sharper, ~14-15 (suggesting a threshold rather than purely linear).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Searching all 8 cells defined by (feature_028, feature_029, feature_018), only the cell (0, 1, 0) shows a non-null feature_013 treatment effect; the other 7 cells all give effects within noise of zero.

analyses.code: For each combination of (feature_028,feature_029,feature_018) in {0,1}^3, ttest_ind pfs_months by feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Full responder subgroup definition: feature_028=0 AND feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033 < 14. In this subgroup feature_013 gives ~+5 months PFS; outside it the effect is ~0.

analyses.code: Define responder = (028==0)&(029==1)&(018==0)&(033<14); compute mean PFS by feature_013 within and outside.
analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup: n=10752 (21.5% of cohort). Within responders: PFS 9.228 (treated) vs 4.284 (control), diff +4.944, p<1e-300. Outside responders: PFS 4.333 (treated) vs 4.317 (control), diff +0.016, p=0.49. Three-way interaction model: const 4.317, feature_013 +0.016 (n.s.), responder -0.033 (n.s.), feature_013:responder +4.928 (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_033 acts as a predictive (treatment-effect-modifier) biomarker only in patients who are otherwise biomarker-positive (feature_028=0, feature_029=1, feature_018=0); in non-responder subgroups feature_033 is irrelevant because the treatment is inactive regardless.

analyses.code: Outside the (028=0,029=1,018=0) subgroup, evaluate feature_013 effect by feature_033 quartile.
analyses.result_summary: Outside the joint biomarker-positive cell, feature_013 effect across f033 quartiles: Q1 -0.020 (p=0.69), Q2 +0.020 (p=0.69), Q3 +0.010 (p=0.84), Q4 +0.005 (p=0.92). All null. Confirms feature_033's predictive role is conditional on the binary biomarker subgroup being met.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal feature_005 (likely a stage/grade variable) is strongly prognostic for PFS but does NOT modify the feature_013 treatment effect (treatment effect is similar in feature_005=0, 1, 2).
The continuous feature_012 (likely age) is strongly prognostic for PFS (positively) but does NOT modify the feature_013 treatment effect (treatment effect is similar across age quartiles).

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_005 in {0,1,2}; compute feature_013 effect.
analyses.result_summary: feature_005=0: tx effect +1.115 (p=6e-156). feature_005=1: +1.093 (p=7e-218). feature_005=2: +1.149 (p=7e-77). Treatment effect essentially identical across the three strata; feature_005 is purely prognostic, not predictive.

analyses.code: Quartile feature_012 and stratify.
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 effect by feature_012 quartile: Q1 +1.162, Q2 +1.119, Q3 +1.065, Q4 +1.039. Stable ~+1.1 across age. Confirms feature_012 is prognostic, not predictive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Strong negative prognostic factors feature_006 and feature_001 do not modify the feature_013 treatment effect: treatment effect should be similar in their positive and negative strata.

analyses.code: Stratified t-test of feature_013 effect by feature_006 and feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0: tx effect +1.096; feature_006=1: +1.102 (essentially identical). feature_001=0: +1.099; feature_001=1: +1.058. Both features are purely prognostic; they do not modulate the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among ALL binary features in the dataset, only feature_013 displays a treatment-like heterogeneity pattern (huge effect inside the joint biomarker-positive subgroup, near-zero outside). No other binary feature has comparable subgroup-conditional behavior.

analyses.code: For each binary feature f: compare effect inside (028=0,029=1,018=0,f033<12) vs outside.
analyses.result_summary: Heterogeneity (effect inside - effect outside the joint biomarker subgroup) for each binary feature: feature_013 +4.73 (uniquely large); feature_009 -0.55 (small effect, low prevalence 2.5%); all others |het|<0.20. No second treatment is hidden among the binary features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A joint additive interaction model with feature_013 main effect and four interactions (feature_028, feature_029, feature_018, feature_033) captures the treatment-effect heterogeneity well, with all four modifiers contributing independently.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013 + feature_028 + feature_029 + feature_018 + feature_033 + feature_013:feature_028 + feature_013:feature_029 + feature_013:feature_018 + feature_013:feature_033.
analyses.result_summary: All four biomarker MAIN effects are non-significant (p>0.66) — they have NO prognostic effect — but all four INTERACTIONS with feature_013 are highly significant: feature_013 main +1.619 (p<1e-300), feature_013:feature_028 -1.589, feature_013:feature_029 +1.550, feature_013:feature_018 -1.337, feature_013:feature_033 -0.052/unit. Treatment effect formula: ~1.62 - 1.59*f028 + 1.55*f029 - 1.34*f018 - 0.052*f033. Maximum effect at (028=0,029=1,018=0,f033=0): ~+3.2 months at average f033, scaling to ~+5 months at f033 ~ 5.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Optimal cutpoint for feature_033 to define the responder subgroup is approximately 14: at thresholds <14, the treatment effect within the joint biomarker-positive subgroup is stable at ~+4.95 months; the contrast vs above-threshold patients (~0 effect) peaks at threshold 14.

analyses.code: Sweep feature_033 thresholds 8..21 in 0.5 steps within (028=0,029=1,018=0); compute effect below vs above and the difference.
analyses.result_summary: Effect_below stable at ~+4.95 (with N growing from 5150 at thresh=8 to 11126 at thresh=14.5); effect_above declines from +2.14 (thresh 8) to ~0 (thresh 14); difference peaks at thresh ~14 with diff = +4.88. Optimal cutpoint ~14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 looks like a partial proxy for the responder subgroup: its prevalence is much higher inside responders (85%) than outside (58%), and the subgroup of (feature_023=1 AND feature_013=1) shows large PFS gain — but the biological 'switch' is not feature_023 itself, it is the feature_028/029/018/033 quartet.

analyses.code: Stratify feature_023 effect by feature_013, then within responder subgroup test feature_023:feature_013 interaction.
analyses.result_summary: feature_023 'effect' is fully explained by feature_013 status: in feature_013=0 patients, feature_023 effect on PFS is -0.020 (p=0.41, null); in feature_013=1 patients, +1.072 (p=5e-134). And inside the established responder subgroup (028=0,029=1,018=0,f033<14), feature_023 has only a marginal additional interaction with feature_013 (coef +0.366, p=0.003). The feature_023 'main effect' is an artifact of its correlation with the joint biomarker-positive subgroup; the canonical responder definition does not need feature_023.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, no other variable substantially modifies the treatment effect (i.e., the responder subgroup definition is essentially complete).

analyses.code: Within responder subgroup, screen all remaining features for additional feature_013 interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Top within-responder modifiers: feature_023 (coef +0.37, p=0.003), feature_012 (coef +0.062 per SD, p=0.009), feature_004 (+0.10, p=0.018), feature_006 (-0.18, p=0.048). None reach the magnitude of the primary modifiers, and most p-values are not impressive given n=10752 — these are minor, not core, modifiers. The subgroup definition (028=0, 029=1, 018=0, f033<14) is essentially complete.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, treatment effect is approximately uniform across feature_005 (stage), feature_012 (age), feature_006, feature_001 — i.e., classical clinical prognostic factors do not gate the response, only the four biomarkers do.

analyses.code: Within responder subgroup, ttest_ind feature_013 effect stratified by feature_005, by feature_012 quartile, and by feature_006/feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, feature_013 effect is large and uniform across all classical prognostic strata (effect ~+5 months in every stratum of feature_005, feature_012 quartile, feature_006, feature_001). The four biomarkers (028, 029, 018, 033) fully define who responds; once a patient is in the responder subgroup, conventional prognosis does not change whether the treatment helps them.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT-HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: feature_013 is an active treatment that PROLONGS pfs_months (positive effect direction) ONLY in patients meeting all four biomarker criteria simultaneously: feature_028=0 AND feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033 < ~14. In this responder subgroup (~21.5% of the cohort, n=10752) the treatment adds ~+4.94 months to mean PFS (9.23 vs 4.28). In the remaining ~78.5% of patients, the treatment has no detectable effect on PFS (+0.016 months, p=0.49). The four biomarkers are predictive (treatment-effect modifying) and not prognostic — none of them is independently associated with PFS in untreated patients.

FINAL PROGNOSTIC HYPOTHESIS: Independent prognostic factors for shorter PFS (regardless of treatment) are: higher feature_005 (ordinal, stage-like, ~-1.17 months per level), feature_006=1 (~-1.55 months), feature_001=1 (~-1.05 months), feature_028=1 (~-0.58 months), feature_018=1 (~-0.47 months), higher feature_004 (-0.077/unit), higher feature_033 (-0.019/unit). Independent prognostic factors for longer PFS are: higher feature_012 (+0.176 per unit, the dominant predictor), feature_029=1 (+0.53), higher feature_024 (+0.48). R^2 of the full additive prognostic model is 0.79.

analyses.code: Final confirmatory: define responder = (feature_028==0)&(feature_029==1)&(feature_018==0)&(feature_033<14); ttest pfs_months by feature_013 within and outside.
analyses.result_summary: Within responder (n=10752): treated mean 9.228, control 4.284, effect +4.944, p<1e-300. Outside responder (n=39248): treated 4.333, control 4.317, effect +0.016, p=0.49. The contrast in treatment effects (+4.93, p<1e-300) confirms the responder subgroup definition.

analyses.code: Final OLS with all 37 features, plus four feature_013-interactions to verify nothing else.
analyses.result_summary: Full multivariable model R^2 = 0.79; principal independent prognostic effects retained as listed in h25.2. Interaction terms confirm feature_013's gating biomarkers as the only treatment effect modifiers.
